Clause 1: Competitors must note that under sub-article 15.2, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 6.

title: Article 39.1 - Drive-Through Penalty variation 4
description: The driver must enter the pit lane, drive through it at the speed limit, and rejoin the track without stopping at their pit box. Clause 2: Competitors must note that under sub-article 39.1, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 9.

title: Article 47.3 - Personnel Safety variation 13
description: All pit crew members working on the car during a pit stop must wear approved helmets, fire-resistant suits, and safety footwear. Clause 5: Competitors must note that under sub-article 47.3, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 4.

title: Article 55.5 - Drive-Through Penalty variation 5

title: Article 53.1 - 5-Second Time Penalty variation 1
description: The driver must stop in their pit grid for 5 seconds before any work can be carried out. If no pit stop is made, 5 seconds is added to their total race time. title: Article 35.8 - Overtaking variation 5
description: Overtaking is strictly prohibited under VSC conditions, except if a driver slows down with an obvious technical problem or enters the pit lane. Clause 5: Competitors must note that under sub-article 35.8, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 1.

title: Article 11.8 - Grid Place Penalty variation 9
description: Applied to the next race start positions. Often given for power unit or gearbox changes exceeding the seasonal allocation. Clause 2: Competitors must note that under sub-article 11.8, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 4.

title: Article 41.4 - Service Limits variation 18
description: No modifications or mechanical alterations can be made to the car during a routine tire pit stop, other than front wing angle adjustments. Clause 5: Competitors must note that under sub-article 41.4, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 7.

title: Article 10.8 - Speed Limit variation 20
description: Drivers must comply with the minimum time limits (delta times) set by the FIA ECU in each marshalling sector. Speeds are typically reduced by 35%. Clause 1: Competitors must note that under sub-article 10.8, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 5.

title: Article 32.1 - Red Flag variation 1
description: Indicates immediate suspension of the session. Drivers must slow down immediately and proceed to the pit lane at a safe speed. Clause 2: Competitors must note that under sub-article 32.1, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 8.

title: Article 28.3 - Deployment variation 1
description: The VSC may be deployed when double waved yellow flags are needed in any section of the track and competitors may be in danger but the circumstances do not warrant the use of the safety car itself. Clause 4: Competitors must note that under sub-article 28.3, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 5.

title: Article 12.1 - Speed Limit variation 1
description: The pit lane speed limit is set at 80 km/h for most circuits, but may be reduced to 60 km/h at tighter tracks like Monaco for safety reasons. Clause 3: Competitors must note that under sub-article 12.1, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 2.

title: Article 53.2 - Tire Change variation 9
description: Each team must change all four wheels during a pit stop. Mixed compound usage is forbidden; all four tires must be of the identical compound specifications. Clause 2: Competitors must note that under sub-article 53.2, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 5.

title: Article 15.8 - Black-and-White Flag variation 16
description: A warning to a driver for unsportsmanlike behavior or repeated track limits violations. Clause 1: Competitors must note that under sub-article 15.8, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 7.

description: Drivers must comply with the minimum time limits (delta times) set by the FIA ECU in each marshalling sector. Speeds are typically reduced by 35%. title: Article 44.6 - Definition variation 2
description: Track limits are defined by the white lines bordering the track. A driver is judged to have left the track if no part of the car remains in contact with the track. Clause 4: Competitors must note that under sub-article 44.6, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 5.

title: Article 35.7 - Violations variation 20
description: Drivers who repeatedly cross track limits without a justifiable reason will receive warnings. After three warnings, a black-and-white flag is shown, and further violations incur a 5-second penalty. Clause 3: Competitors must note that under sub-article 35.7, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 6.

description: Indicates immediate suspension of the session. Drivers must slow down immediately and proceed to the pit lane at a safe speed. title: Article 55.9 - Deployment Procedure variation 14
description: The Clerk of the Course may deploy the safety car if a track blockage or dangerous condition arises. All drivers must reduce speed and line up behind the safety car. Clause 4: Competitors must note that under sub-article 55.9, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 5.

title: Article 39.1 - Ending VSC variation 5
description: When the VSC ending message is displayed, the track will go green after a random 10-15 second countdown. Clause 1: Competitors must note that under sub-article 39.1, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 5.

title: Article 52.3 - Q1 Format variation 14
description: An 18-minute session where all 20 cars participate. The slowest 5 cars are eliminated and take grid positions 16-20. Clause 5: Competitors must note that under sub-article 52.3, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 2.

title: Article 35.3 - Q1 Format variation 1
description: An 18-minute session where all 20 cars participate. The slowest 5 cars are eliminated and take grid positions 16-20. title: Article 24.2 - Safety Car Ending variation 20
description: When the safety car enters the pit lane, cars may resume racing only after they cross the First Safety Car Line. Clause 5: Competitors must note that under sub-article 24.2, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 4.

title: Article 27.9 - Exceptions variation 16
description: A driver will not be penalized if they are forced off the track by another competitor, subject to review by the stewards. Clause 5: Competitors must note that under sub-article 27.9, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 12.

title: Article 44.7 - Advantage gained variation 15
description: If a driver leaves the track, they must not gain a lasting advantage. Any positions gained must be given back immediately. Clause 5: Competitors must note that under sub-article 44.7, failure to comply will result in an immediate referral to the stewards under Sporting Regulation Appendix 4.
